sglang.title: [AMD] Fix Tensor Memory Aliasing
sglang.author: bingxche
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
PR #19263 switched from forward_native (returns fresh torch.cat tensors) to forward_cuda (returns the same in-place-modified tensors). On ROCm, PyTorch explicitly checks for and rejects self-aliased tensor writes, surfacing the latent bug.
Errors:

https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/ac

sglang.title: fix: image URL in notebook to use raw.githubusercontent.com
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Multiple doc notebooks use GitHub blob URLs (github.com/...?raw=true) to fetch images, which can return HTML instead of image bytes in CI due to rate limiting or redirect issues, causing PIL.UnidentifiedImageError and failing the make compile step. Additionally, the same URL is hardcoded

Summary

Add test/show_partitions.py that prints which test files land in which partition for every CI stage
Parses stage configs (hw, suite, partition_size) directly from pr-test.yml via regex, so partition counts never go out of sync with the workflow
Uses the same deterministic LPT algorithm as r

When running DeepSeek-R1 FP8 on MI300x, vLLM crashed during model initialization with:
ValueError: Unable to find matching target for model.layers.0.self_attn.fused_qkv_a_proj in the compressed-tensors config.

The compressed-tensors quantization config matches layers by checking if a target

vllm.title: [V0 Deprecation] Remove unused swap_space parameter
vllm.author: majiayu000
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Remove the unused swap_space parameter from the codebase. This parameter was only used for best_of sampling which was removed in V1 (#13997, #29090). In the V1 engine, num_cpu_blocks is hardcoded to 0 (v1/engine/core.py:287), confirming swap space was never allocated or used.
Picks up the ab

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove duplicate parser registration
vllm.author: taneem-ibrahim
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Removed duplicate ParserManager.register_lazy_module() call for minimax_m2 in vllm/parser/__init__.py
The parser was registered twice: once via a direct call and again via the register_lazy_parsers() function that iterates _PARSERS_TO_REGISTER

vllm.title: Adding support to Sarvam's MoE models
vllm.author: rahul-sarvam
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for Sarvam MoE model executors in vLLM.
SarvamMoEForCausalLM implements a standard MoE architecture with conventional MHA, while SarvamMLAForCausalLM implements an MoE variant using MLA. The implementation reuses existing vLLM primitives (MLA modules, fused MoE, TP/PP support, a

vllm.title: [UX][Startup] Account for CUDA graphs during memory profiling
vllm.author: MatthewBonanni
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Memory profiling during startup doesn't account for all uses of GPU memory, hence the need for --gpu-memory-utilization. One notable miss is the size of the CUDA graphs, which are captured after memory profiling due to their dependence on the KV cache. This can lead to oversized KV cache and

vllm.title: [Bugfix][LMCache][KVConnector] fix potential memory leak in LMCache multiprocess mode
vllm.author: royyhuang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix a potential memory leak in LMCache multiprocess (MP) mode caused by lookup locks not being freed for chunks that vLLM computes itself rather than retrieving from LMCache.
When a prefetch lookup returns hits but vLLM decides to compute some (or all) of those blocks itself, the correspondi
